**The Most Honourable Order of Seri Paduka Mahkota Brunei**[[1]](#cite_note-1) ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Darjah Seri Paduka Mahkota Brunei Yang Amat Mulia*), also translated as **The Most Honourable Order of the Crown of Brunei**,[[2]](#cite_note-Medals.org.uk-2) is an [order](/wiki/Order_(decoration) "Order (decoration)") of [Brunei Darussalam](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"). It was established on 1 March 1954 by [Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Sultan_Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III").[[2]](#cite_note-Medals.org.uk-2)

## Current classes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Seri_Paduka_Mahkota_Brunei&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Current classes")]

The three classes of appointment to the Order are, from highest grade to lowest grade:

| Class | [Post-nominal](/wiki/Post-nominal_letters "Post-nominal letters") | [Title](/wiki/Malay_styles_and_titles "Malay styles and titles") | [Ribbon bar](/wiki/Ribbon_bar "Ribbon bar") |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| First Class | SPMB | Dato Seri Paduka |  |
| Second Class | DPMB | Dato Paduka |  |
| Third Class | SMB | — |  |

## Description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Seri_Paduka_Mahkota_Brunei&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Description")]

The majority of the state crest is shown on the collar chain's front and back, and two of the crowns are arranged in an octagonal shape of black enamel. In the other links, a portion of the state crest is shown significantly differently with white crescent moons, crossed [kris](/wiki/Kris "Kris"), and crossed flags.[[3]](#cite_note-:8-3) The first class included a 62 millimetres (2.4 in) silver-gilt, silver-and-enamel neck badge; an 87 millimetres (3.4 in) silver-gilt, silver-and-enamel star with a maker's cartouche on the reverse in excellent condition; neck ribands; and a fitted box of issuance.[[4]](#cite_note-:31-4)

## Recipients

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Seri_Paduka_Mahkota_Brunei&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Recipients")]

This order was established to honor those who have helped the Sultan in exceptional or significant ways.[[3]](#cite_note-:8-3)

### First Class

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Seri_Paduka_Mahkota_Brunei&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: First Class")]

Typical Bruneian recipients include Deputy Ministers, the Chief Justice, and the Attorney General.
